concepts.finding: Dense networks contain subnetworks (winning tickets) that, when trained in isolation from their initializations, reach accuracy comparable to the original network in similar iterations, typically 10-20% of original size.
concepts.implication: Finding sparse trainable subnetworks means we could train and serve much smaller search models from scratch if winning tickets are identified early.
concepts.open_question: Can lottery ticket subnetworks be identified early in training for search models, enabling training of sparse models from scratch?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Lottery ticket subnetworks identified at initialization for a query intent classifier will achieve >95% of full model accuracy with only 20% of the parameters.
